zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Sidney於大約1482年出生在英國肯特書爾斯特時，他的父親Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst36歲，母親Lady Anne Brandon29歲。他於1517年與Lady Anne Pakenham在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1554年2月10日在英國英格蘭肯特書爾斯特享年73歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特書爾斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Sidney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Sidney Farm in Alfold (Surrey), from Old English sīd ‘large, spacious, long’ (dative sīdan) + ēg ‘island’.
possibly also a habitational name from any of the places called Saint-Denis in Normandy, named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Dionysius (see   Dennis  ).

variants.name: Dennis
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Denis(e), Dinis(e), vernacular forms of Latin Dionysius, Greek Dionysios ‘(follower) of Dionysos’, an eastern god introduced to the classical pantheon at a relatively late date and bearing a name of probably Semitic origin. The name was borne by various early Christian saints, including Saint Denis, the martyred 3rd-century bishop of Paris who became the patron saint of France. From the 12th century onward, the popularity of the name in England (and of its cognates, mainly Denis , in the continental Europe) seems to have been largely due to French influence. The feminine form Dionysia (in vernacular English likewise Den(n)is) is also found, and some examples of the surname may originate from it.
English: from Middle English deneis, a variant of danais ‘Danish’, with the vowel influenced by Middle English denshe (Old English denisc). Compare Dence .

Irish (mainly Dublin and Cork): of the same origin as 1 and 2 above, sometimes an alternative form to Donohue but more often to McDonough , since the personal name Donnchadh was Anglicized as Donough or Denis.

variants.name: Sydney
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): variant of   Sidney  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Sidney was born about 1482, in Penshurst, Kent, England, his father, Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst, was 36 and his mother, Lady Anne Brandon, was 29. He married Lady Anne Pakenham in 1517, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 6 daughters. He died on 10 February 1554, in Penshurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Penshurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Sidney est né environ 1482, à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Lady Anne Brandon, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Lady Anne Pakenham en 1517, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 10 février 1554, à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Penshurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Sidneyが約1482年にイギリスケントペンシュルストで生まれた時，父Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurstは36歳，母Lady Anne Brandonは29歳であった。1517年にイギリスでLady Anne Pakenhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼は1554年2月10日にイギリスイギリスケントペンシュルストで73歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントペンシュルストにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Sidney(이)가 대략 1482년 영국 켄트 펜슈르스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Anne Brandon(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1517년 영국에서 Lady Anne Pakenham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1554년 2월 10일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 펜슈르스트에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 펜슈르스트에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Anne Pakenham em 1517, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 6 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Sidney родился около 1482 г. в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Nicholas John Sydney of Penshurst было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Anne Brandon, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Anne Pakenham в 1517 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 6 дочерей. Он умер 10 февраля 1554 г. в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и был похоронен в местности пеншурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
